contract.name: v2/financial_balances/agencies.md
contract.description: Returns aggregated budget authority, obligations, and outlays for a specified funding agency and fiscal year. Responses always include pagination metadata even though only a single balance record has been observed.

fiscal_year.description: Fiscal year to summarize balances for the funding agency.
fiscal_year.constraints: Required. Must be digits representing a fiscal year; missing yields HTTP 400. Non-numeric or decimal strings trigger 500 HTML errors. Negative or far-future years are accepted but return empty results.

funding_agency_id.description: USAspending funding agency identifier (agency_id from the toptier reference list).
funding_agency_id.constraints: Required. Must be digits representing a funding agency ID; blank values are treated as missing (400). Non-numeric or decimal strings trigger 500 HTML errors. Zero or negative IDs are accepted but return empty results.

limit.description: Page size for the response (responses currently contain at most one record).
limit.constraints: Optional. Defaults to 100. Values less than 1 are coerced to 1, values above 500 are capped at 500 with no warning. Non-numeric or decimal strings trigger 500 HTML errors.

page.constraints: Optional. Defaults to 1. Values less than 1 are normalized to 1. Large page values return empty results while count remains unchanged. Non-numeric or decimal strings trigger 500 HTML errors.

page_metadata.description: Pagination metadata including count (integer), page (integer), has_next_page (boolean), has_previous_page (boolean), next/current/previous (URL strings or null). Requests for page numbers beyond available data preserve the requested page and may set has_previous_page true even when count is 0.

results.description: Array containing zero or one FinancialBalance object. Each FinancialBalance has string fields budget_authority_amount, obligated_amount, and outlay_amount representing aggregated totals for the requested agency and fiscal year.

body.detail: Missing one or more required query parameters: fiscal_year, funding_agency_id

quirks: Responses always include page_metadata with pagination URLs even though the public docs only mention results.
Endpoint supports page and limit query parameters; limit defaults to 100, coerces values below 1 to 1, and silently caps values above 500.
Invalid numeric formats (alphabetic, decimal, or blank strings) trigger 500 responses with HTML bodies instead of JSON validation errors.
Unknown, zero, or negative funding_agency_id values return 200 with empty results, making typos easy to miss.
page_metadata.current echoes any unexpected query parameters exactly as submitted.
Observed responses contain at most one FinancialBalance entry; additional pages return empty arrays while count stays 0 or 1.
Requesting page numbers greater than available results may set has_previous_page true even when the dataset is empty.
risks: Clients must handle 500 HTML error pages for malformed query input instead of structured JSON errors.
Silent coercion of page and limit parameters can hide integration mistakes and unexpectedly alter pagination.
200 responses with empty results for invalid funding_agency_id values may mask typos or outdated identifiers.
Pagination metadata may indicate has_previous_page true even when count is 0, so navigation logic must account for misleading flags.
gaps: Valid funding_agency_id values were not enumerated; use the references endpoint to confirm identifiers.

probes.notes: pass - baseline call confirming successful response and pagination metadata
pass - example fiscal year showing non-empty balances

fail - requesting page 2 returns empty results while count stays 1 and has_previous_page is true

fail - page metadata shows has_previous_page true even though count is 0

mismatches: Documentation omits pagination metadata, but the endpoint always returns page_metadata and supports page/limit controls.
Documentation implies type validation for required numerics, yet malformed numeric inputs produce 500 HTML responses instead of JSON validation errors.
gaps: Valid funding_agency_id values were not enumerated; use the references endpoint to confirm identifiers.
risks: Clients must handle 500 HTML error pages for malformed query input instead of structured JSON errors.
Silent coercion of page and limit parameters can hide integration mistakes and unexpectedly alter pagination.
200 responses with empty results for invalid funding_agency_id values may mask typos or outdated identifiers.
Pagination metadata may indicate has_previous_page true even when count is 0, so navigation logic must account for misleading flags.
